rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SALLY CHRISTINE OWENSBY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, there being no objections to the petition as supplemented by the personal representative. The personal representative needed just to verify the "declaration" filed by her attorney on 9/20/22. Her own "declaration" was not filed until 10/25/22. There was no need to attach a copy of the petition or the attorney's "declaration" to her "declaration". Furthermore, her attorney's declaration filed on 9/20/22 referred to a petition for preliminary distribution; the Court's records show that that petition was missing pages 3-5 and was never amended or adjudicated (only some disbursements were ordered). A petition should stand on its own and not rely on previously filed petitions, especially incomplete ones. Issues raised by an examiner are not "objections" to a pleading; they merely point out defects with a petition or missing or incomplete information. The hearing was continued because the attorney, not his fiduciary client, filed a "declaration" to address issues with the fiduciary's petition, when a fiduciary is required by the Probate Code and California Rules of Court to sign and verify her own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment thereto. Costs to be reimbursed should show the payee and the actual date such cost was incurred. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSIE MAE RUSH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the administrator did not allege whether or not the decedent was the surviving spouse or domestic partner of a person who received benefits that would require notice under Probate Code Section 9202(a), and the accounting schedules were not sufficiently detailed, showing the SOURCE of receipts or the PAYEES of disbursements with full descriptions. Findings and the order should be consistent with the report and prayer, not findings AND order. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID ALLEN HERRING
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/21/22. Counsel to supplement the following issues: (1) an authority under which the court can approved statutory compensation payable to counsel when the estate has "0" value per Inventory and Appraisal. Petitioner needs to file supplemental Inventory and Appraisal. Prob. Code 8801. Appears the assets of the inheritance from the decedent's mother's estate are subject to appraisal by the probate referee. Prob. Code 8903(b); (2) a copy of the deceased mother's two IRA accounts to show predeceased designated beneficiary's 20% to be distributed to the predeceased beneficiary's estate, not to be allocated to the surviving beneficiaries or to the account owner's estate; and (3) Proposed Order must be submitted. Information must be provided at least 7 days prior to the next hearing.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL LARRY MCLEAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/28/22. No courtesy copies of papers filed since the hearing on 8/31/22 have been received by the examiner as of 11/3/22. Still no proof of publication. It appears that Notice of Petition to Administer Estate was mailed on 10/27/22 for this hearing, which was not at least 15 days' notice. The Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is missing the telephone number in item 2. No supplement to the petition has been filed yet to identify the parents of the decedent and to set forth their dates of death. Item 6f is not the first box that applies if the petitioner was the decedent's brother. No waivers of bond have been filed. A Notice of Proposed Action was filed on 10/14/22, but there is no personal representative appointed yet to take any action. Courtesy copies of ALL papers filed to date plus the supplement to the petition, proof of service of notice of the 11/28/22 hearing, indicating that notice was mailed at least 15 days before 11/28/22, and waivers of bond must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 11/18/22 to avoid another continuance. If not all heirs waive bond, bond will be required. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA JEAN WASH RILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Tanya Rockett, executor named in the decedent's will, objected to the petitioner's appointment, but no competing petition has been filed to claim her priority. 2) The petitioner filed creditor claims individually and as trustee of the devisee trust against this estate, even though a creditor "claim" does not include a dispute regarding title of a decedent to specific property alleged to be included in the decedent's estate. See Probate Code section 9000(b). 3) Petioner is the trustee of the Survivor's Trust, a sub-trust created by the devisee trust upon the death of the decedent's spouse. The petitioner does not appear to have executed a deed or other instrument to transfer title to the Fell Street property from the Survivor's Trust to the petitioner as a beneficiary, nor has the petitioner opened a new trust case to petition under Probate Code section 17200(b)(4) or (6) for an order ascertaining beneficiaries to whom the Fell Street property shall pass or instructing the petitioner as trustee. 4) Article X-A-2 of the governing trust instrument appears to require specific distribution of the Fell Street property to the decedent and petitioner in equal shares "upon the surviving settlor's death". It is unclear whether the decedent's 50% interest in the property must be first distributed to this probate estate or can be distributed directly to the beneficiaries of the Survivor's Trust named in the power of appointment exercised by the decedent. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/19/22. Still no proposed order or courtesy copies. The proposed order and courtesy copies of all papers filed to date and the Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (and any other papers filed before 12/19/22) must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 12/9/22 to avoid another automatic continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HENRY VARELA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/7/2022. Proof of publication needs to be re-filed as it does not set forth complete notice.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN L. VENTURA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 1/22/2000. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status of closing the estate will be set for 11/20/23. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 11/20/23, no appearance will be required on that date.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SAGE BOWLING AND SKY BOWLING, MINORS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For Order Directing Deposit Of Minors' Funds In Blocked Account
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although no courtesy copy of the consent by the minors' mother was provided. Status of filing receipts for the blocked accounts is set for 12/21/22. If the receipts for the blocked accounts are filed by 12/7/22, no appearance will be required on 12/21/22.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEVEN MAURICE GRAINER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/7/2022 at counsel's request.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NOY DIAZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/07/2022. Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 11/02/2022.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE MARRIAGE OF NICOLE MAPA COATES
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the party to address the following: 1.Documents submitted as a part of court record in foreign language must translated into English. LRSF 14.15 2.Need explanation why this petition is needed. The attached document appears as a copy of the marriage certificate issued by Mexico. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.
